intro: Xiphopteris hecistophylla, also known as the Hairy-leaved Xiphopteris, is a species of fern native to tropical Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Xiphopteris hecistophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Xiphopteris hecistophylla is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
